title: hare krsna hare krsna
translation: Maha-mantra

title: sarva-dharman parityajya
translation: Surrender to Krishna

title: janma karma ca me divyam
translation: Knowing Lord's appearance

title: isvarah paramah krsnah
translation: Supreme Lord is Krishna

word_by_word.meaning: Govinda, giver of pleasure to the senses

title: bahunam janmanam ante
translation: Surrender after many births

word_by_word.meaning: one who is endowed with knowledge
Shree Krishna, the son of Vasudev

title: brahma-bhutah prasannatma
translation: Transcendental platform

title: dehino 'smin yatha dehe
translation: Transmigration of the soul

title: daivi hy esa guna-mayi
translation: Overcoming maya by surrender

word_by_word.meaning: consisting of the three modes of nature
one of God’s energies. It that veils God’s true nature from souls who have not yet attained the eligibility for God-realization

title: man-mana bhava mad-bhakto
translation: Always think of Krishna

title: prakrteh kriyamanani
translation: False ego as doer

word_by_word.meaning: those who are bewildered by the ego and misidentify themselves with the body

title: janmady asya yatah
translation: Brahman as source of all

title: isvarah sarva-bhutanam
translation: Supersoul in the heart

title: sarvasya caham hrdi sannivisto
transliteration: vedaiśh cha sarvair aham eva vedyo
translation: Krishna in everyone's heart

word_by_word.meaning: the knower of the meaning of the Vedas

title: na te viduh svartha-gatim hi visnum
translation: Persons who are strongly entrapped by the consciousness of enjoying material life, and who have therefore accepted as their leader or guru a similar blind man attached to external sense objects, cannot understand that the goal of life is to return home, back to Godhead, and engage in the service of Lord Visnu. As blind men guided by another blind man miss the right path and fall into a ditch, materially attached men led by another materially attached man are bound by the ropes of fruitive labor, which are made of very strong cords, and they continue again and again in materialistic life, suffering the threefold miseries.

word_by_word.meaning: the ultimate goal of life, or their own real interest
being ambitious to enjoy this material world
to the ropes (laws) of material nature

title: athato brahma-jijnasa
translation: Inquiry into Brahman

title: na mam duskrtino mudhah
translation: Four types of miscreants

word_by_word.meaning: one who lazily follows one’s lower nature

title: aham sarvasya prabhavo
translation: Krishna as source of all

word_by_word.meaning: endowed with great faith and devotion

title: na jayate mriyate va kadacin
transliteration: nāyaṁ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ
translation: Eternal nature of the soul

title: ceto-darpana-marjanam
translation: “ ‘Let there be all victory for the chanting of the holy name of Lord Kṛṣṇa, which can cleanse the mirror of the heart and stop the miseries of the blazing fire of material existence. That chanting is the waxing moon that spreads the white lotus of good fortune for all living entities. It is the life and soul of all education. The chanting of the holy name of Kṛṣṇa expands the blissful ocean of transcendental life. It gives a cooling effect to everyone and enables one to taste full nectar at every step.’

word_by_word.meaning: for the congregational chanting of the holy name of Krsna

title: aham brahmasmi
translation: I am Brahman

title: sarvopadhi-vinirmuktam
translation: Freed from all designations

title: sa vai pumsam paro dharmo
translation: The supreme occupation (dharma) for all humanity is that by which men can attain to loving devotional service unto the transcendent Lord. Such devotional service must be unmotivated and uninterrupted to completely satisfy the self.

title: sravanam kirtanam visnoh
translation: Prahlada Maharaja said: Hearing and chanting about the transcendental holy name, form, qualities, paraphernalia and pastimes of Lord Visnu, remembering them, serving the lotus feet of the Lord, offering the Lord respectful worship with sixteen types of paraphernalia, offering prayers to the Lord, becoming His servant, considering the Lord one’s best friend, and surrendering everything unto Him (in other words, serving Him with the body, mind and words)—these nine processes are accepted as pure devotional service. One who has dedicated his life to the service of Krsna through these nine methods should be understood to be the most learned person, for he has acquired complete knowledge.

word_by_word.meaning: offering worship (with sodasopacara, the sixteen kinds of paraphernalia)
unto Lord Visnu (not to anyone else)
unto the Supreme Personality of Godhead

title: nityo nityanam cetanas cetananam
translation: The supreme eternal among eternals

title: mam ca yo 'vyabhicarena
translation: Transcending the three modes

word_by_word.meaning: the three modes of material nature

title: bhoktaram yajna-tapasam
translation: Krishna as supreme enjoyer

title: anyabhilasita-sunyam
translation: Pure devotional service defined

title: harer nama harer nama
translation: Chanting as only means in Kali-yuga

title: manusyanam sahasresu
translation: Rare souls who know Krishna

word_by_word.meaning: of those who have achieved perfection

title: tad-vijnanartham sa gurum evabhigacchet
translation: Approaching a bona fide guru

title: yasyatma-buddhih kunape tri-dhatuke
translation: One who identifies his self as the inert body composed of mucus, bile and air, who assumes his wife and family are permanently his own, who thinks an earthen image or the land of his birth is worshipable, or who sees a place of pilgrimage as merely the water there, but who never identifies himself with, feels kinship with, worships or even visits those who are wise in spiritual truth — such a person is no better than a cow or an ass.

title: evam parampara-praptam
translation: Disciplic succession

word_by_word.meaning: with the long passage of time

title: indriyarthesu vairagyam
translation: Renunciation of sense objects

title: catur-varnyam maya srstam
translation: Four social divisions created by Krishna

title: mamaivamso jiva-loke
translation: Living entities as Krishna's parts

title: yada yada hi dharmasya
translation: Krishna's periodic descents

title: bhaktya mam abhijanati
translation: Knowing Krishna only by devotion

title: na tasya karyam karanam ca vidyate
translation: No work or organ for the Supreme

title: atah sri-krsna namadi
translation: Senses cannot perceive Krishna

title: mayadhyaksena prakrtih
translation: Material nature under Krishna's supervision

title: mattah parataram nanyat
translation: Nothing superior to Krishna

title: vadanti tat tattva-vidas
translation: Learned transcendentalists who know the Absolute Truth call this nondual substance Brahman, Paramatma or Bhagavan.

title: paritranaya sadhunam
translation: Lord's purpose of descent

title: yare dekha tare kaha krsna upadesa
translation: “Instruct everyone to follow the orders of Lord Śrī Kṛṣṇa as they are given in the Bhagavad-gītā and Śrīmad-Bhāgavatam. In this way become a spiritual master and try to liberate everyone in this land.”

word_by_word.meaning: the instruction of the Bhagavad-gita as it is spoken by the Lord or of Srimad-Bhagavatam, which advises one to worship Sri Krsna

title: sarva-yonisu kaunteya
translation: Krishna as seed-giving father

title: ete camsa-kalah pumsam
translation: All of the above-mentioned incarnations are either plenary portions or portions of the plenary portions of the Lord, but Lord Sri Krsna is the original Personality of Godhead. All of them appear on planes whenever there is a disturbance created by the atheists. The Lord incarnates to protect the theists.

title: ye 'nye 'ravindaksa vimukta-maninas
translation: [Someone may say that aside from devotees, who always seek shelter at the Lord’s lotus feet, there are those who are not devotees but who have accepted different processes for attaining salvation. What happens to them? In answer to this question, Lord Brahmā and the other demigods said:] O lotus-eyed Lord, although nondevotees who accept severe austerities and penances to achieve the highest position may think themselves liberated, their intelligence is impure. They fall down from their position of imagined superiority because they have no regard for Your lotus feet.

title: yoginam api sarvesam
translation: Devotee as highest yogi

title: tad viddhi pranipatena
translation: Approaching the spiritual master

word_by_word.meaning: those who have realized the Truth

title: patram puspam phalam toyam
translation: Simple offering with devotion

title: vidya-vinaya-sampanne
translation: Equal vision of the wise

title: param brahma param dhama
translation: Arjuna's declaration of Krishna

title: trnad api sunicena
translation: “ ‘One who thinks himself lower than the grass, who is more tolerant than a tree, and who does not expect personal honor but is always prepared to give all respect to others can very easily always chant the holy name of the Lord.’

word_by_word.meaning: without being puffed up by false pride
the holy name of the Lord

title: isavasyam idam sarvam
translation: Everything belongs to the Lord

title: tesam satata-yuktanam
translation: Krishna gives intelligence to devotees

title: dharmah svanusthitah pumsam
translation: The occupational activities a man performs according to his own position are only so much useless labor if they do not provoke attraction for the message of the Personality of Godhead.

word_by_word.meaning: executed in terms of one’s own position

title: mayy asakta-manah partha
translation: Hearing from Krishna attentively

title: purusah prakrti-stho hi
translation: Soul in material nature

title: yajnarthat karmano 'yatra
translation: Work for Vishnu to avoid bondage

title: bhumir apo 'nalo vayuh
translation: Eight material elements

title: nayam deho deha-bhajam nrloke
translation: Lord Rsabhadeva told His sons: My dear boys, of all the living entities who have accepted material bodies in this world, one who has been awarded this human form should not work hard day and night simply for sense gratification, which is available even for dogs and hogs that eat stool. One should engage in penance and austerity to attain the divine position of devotional service. By such activity, one’s heart is purified, and when one attains this position, he attains eternal, blissful life, which is transcendental to material happiness and which continues forever.

word_by_word.meaning: of all living entities who have accepted material bodies

title: janmady asya yato 'nvayad
transliteration: svarāṭtene brahma hṛdā ya ādikavaye muhyanti yatsūrayaḥ
svena sadā nirastakuhakaṁ satyaṁ paraṁ dhīmahi
translation: O my Lord, Sri Krsna, son of Vasudeva, O all-pervading Personality of Godhead, I offer my respectful obeisances unto You. I meditate upon Lord Sri Krsna because He is the Absolute Truth and the primeval cause of all causes of the creation, sustenance and destruction of the manifested universes. He is directly and indirectly conscious of all manifestations, and He is independent because there is no other cause beyond Him. It is He only who first imparted the Vedic knowledge unto the heart of Brahmaji, the original living being. By Him even the great sages and demigods are placed into illusion, as one is bewildered by the illusory representations of water seen in fire, or land seen on water. Only because of Him do the material universes, temporarily manifested by the reactions of the three modes of nature, appear factual, although they are unreal. I therefore meditate upon Him, Lord Sri Krsna, who is eternally existent in the transcendental abode, which is forever free from the illusory representations of the material world. I meditate upon Him, for He is the Absolute Truth.

word_by_word.meaning: unto Vasudeva (the son of Vasudeva), or Lord Sri Krsna, the primeval Lord
three modes of creation, creative faculties

title: mam upetya punar janma
translation: No return from Krishna's abode

title: imam vivasvate yogam
translation: Bhagavad-gita spoken to sun-god

word_by_word.meaning: the Supreme Lord Shree Krishna said
to Manu, the original progenitor of humankind
to Ikshvaku, first king of the Solar dynasty

title: yasyasti bhaktir bhagavaty akincana
translation: All the demigods and their exalted qualities, such as religion, knowledge and renunciation, become manifest in the body of one who has developed unalloyed devotion for the Supreme Personality of Godhead, Vasudeva. On the other hand, a person devoid of devotional service and engaged in material activities has no good qualities. Even if he is adept at the practice of mystic yoga or the honest endeavor of maintaining his family and relatives, he must be driven by his own mental speculations and must engage in the service of the Lord’s external energy. How can there be any good qualities in such a man?

word_by_word.meaning: to the Supreme Personality of Godhead
unto the Supreme Personality of Godhead
of a person who is not devoted

title: avajananti mam mudha
translation: Fools deride Krishna's form

title: yanti deva-vrata devan
translation: Destination according to worship

title: premanjana-cchurita bhakti-vilocanena
translation: Seeing Krishna with eyes of devotion

title: srnvatam sva-kathah krsnah
translation: Sri Krsna, the Personality of Godhead, who is the Paramatma (Supersoul) in everyone’s heart and the benefactor of the truthful devotee, cleanses desire for material enjoyment from the heart of the devotee who has developed the urge to hear His messages, which are in themselves virtuous when properly heard and chanted.

word_by_word.meaning: those who have developed the urge to hear the message of

title: matir na krsne paratah svato va
translation: Prahlada Maharaja replied: Because of their uncontrolled senses, persons too addicted to materialistic life make progress toward hellish conditions and repeatedly chew that which has already been chewed. Their inclinations toward Krsna are never aroused, either by the instructions of others, by their own efforts, or by a combination of both.

word_by_word.meaning: of persons too addicted to the materialistic, bodily conception of life

title: bharata-bhumite haila manusya-janma-yara
translation: “One who has taken his birth as a human being in the land of India [Bhārata-varṣa] should make his life successful and work for the benefit of all other people.

title: krsna-varnam tvisakrsnam
translation: In the Age of Kali, intelligent persons perform congregational chanting to worship the incarnation of Godhead who constantly sings the names of Kṛṣṇa. Although His complexion is not blackish, He is Kṛṣṇa Himself. He is accompanied by His associates, servants, weapons and confidential companions.

title: advaitam acyutam anadim ananta-rupam
translation: Krishna's unlimited forms

title: ye yatha mam prapadyante
translation: Krishna responds to all surrender

title: tarko 'pratisthah srutayo vibhinna
translation: Need for a great devotee's conclusion

title: karmana daiva-netrena
translation: The Personality of Godhead said: Under the supervision of the Supreme Lord and according to the result of his work, the living entity, the soul, is made to enter into the womb of a woman through the particle of male semen to assume a particular type of body.

word_by_word.meaning: the Supreme Personality of Godhead said
under the supervision of the Lord

title: jivera svarupa haya--krsnera nitya-dasa
translation: “It is the living entity’s constitutional position to be an eternal servant of Kṛṣṇa because he is the marginal energy of Kṛṣṇa and a manifestation simultaneously one with and different from the Lord, like a molecular particle of sunshine or fire. Kṛṣṇa has three varieties of energy.

word_by_word.meaning: part and parcel of the sun

title: bhuta-gramah sa evayam
translation: Repeated creation and dissolution

title: mam hi partha vyapasritya
translation: Anyone can return to Krishna

title: atah pumbhir dvija-srestha
translation: O best among the twice-born, it is therefore concluded that the highest perfection one can achieve by discharging the duties prescribed for one’s own occupation according to caste divisions and orders of life is to please the Personality of Godhead.

word_by_word.meaning: the institution of four castes and four orders of life

title: ananda-cinmaya-rasa-pratibhavitabhis
translation: Gopis as spiritual energy

title: dharmam tu saksat bhagavat-pranitam
translation: Real religious principles are enacted by the Supreme Personality of Godhead. Although fully situated in the mode of goodness, even the great rsis who occupy the topmost planes cannot ascertain the real religious principles, nor can the demigods or the leaders of Siddhaloka, to say nothing of the asuras, ordinary human beings, Vidyadharas and Caranas.

word_by_word.meaning: real religious principles, or bona fide laws of religion
by the Supreme Personality of Godhead
the great rsis such as Bhrgu
the inhabitants of Bhurloka, the human beings
the lesser demigods known as Vidyadharas
the residents of the planes where people are by nature great musicians and singers

title: maya tatam idam sarvam
translation: Krishna pervades everything unmanifest

title: brahmanda bhramite kona bhagyavan jiva
translation: “According to their karma, all living entities are wandering throughout the entire universe. Some of them are being elevated to the upper planetary systems, and some are going down into the lower planetary systems. Out of many millions of wandering living entities, one who is very fortunate gets an opportunity to associate with a bona fide spiritual master by the grace of Kṛṣṇa. By the mercy of both Kṛṣṇa and the spiritual master, such a person receives the seed of the creeper of devotional service.

word_by_word.meaning: of the creeper of devotional service

title: nunam pramattah kurute vikarma
translation: When a person considers sense gratification the aim of life, he certainly becomes mad after materialistic living and engages in all kinds of sinful activity. He does not know that due to his past misdeeds he has already received a body which, although temporary, is the cause of his misery. Actually the living entity should not have taken on a material body, but he has been awarded the material body for sense gratification. Therefore I think it not befitting an intelligent man to involve himself again in the activities of sense gratification by which he perpetually gets material bodies one after another.

word_by_word.meaning: sinful activities forbidden in the scriptures

title: pumsah striya mithuni-bhavam etam
translation: The attraction between male and female is the basic principle of material existence. On the basis of this misconception, which ties together the hearts of the male and female, one becomes attracted to his body, home, property, children, relatives and wealth. In this way one increases life’s illusions and thinks in terms of I and mine

title: eko 'py asau racayitum jagad-anda-kotim
translation: Govinda expands into all universes

title: ahara-nidra-bhaya-maithunam ca
translation: Animals also eat sleep mate defend

title: na tad bhasayate suryo
translation: No need of sun in Krishna's abode

title: dharmah projjhita-kaitavo 'tra
translation: Completely rejecting all religious activities which are materially motivated, this Bhagavata Purana propounds the highest truth, which is understandable by those devotees who are fully pure in heart. The highest truth is reality distinguished from illusion for the welfare of all. Such truth uproots the threefold miseries. This beautiful Bhagavatam, compiled by the great sage Vyasadeva (in his maturity), is sufficient in itself for God realization. What is the need of any other scripture? As soon as one attentively and submissively hears the message of Bhagavatam, by this culture of knowledge the Supreme Lord is established within his heart.

word_by_word.meaning: of the one-hundred-percent pure in heart

title: kaumara acaret prajno
translation: Prahlada Maharaja said: One who is sufficiently intelligent should use the human form of body from the very beginning of life—in other words, from the tender age of childhood—to practice the activities of devotional service, giving up all other engagements. The human body is most rarely achieved, and although temporary like other bodies, it is meaningful because in human life one can perform devotional service. Even a slight amount of sincere devotional service can give one complete perfection.

word_by_word.meaning: in the tender age of childhood
which are devotional service to the Supreme Personality of Godhead

title: vasudeve bhagavati
translation: By rendering devotional service unto the Personality of Godhead, Sri Krsna, one immediately acquires causeless knowledge and detachment from the world.

title: samo damas tapah saucam
translation: Brahminical qualities

title: yesam tv anta-gatam papam
translation: Pious persons free from sin worship

title: tasmad gurum prapadyeta
translation: Therefore any person who seriously desires real happiness must seek a bona fide spiritual master and take shelter of him by initiation. The qualification of the bona fide guru is that he has realized the conclusions of the scriptures by deliberation and is able to convince others of these conclusions. Such great personalities, who have taken shelter of the Supreme Godhead, leaving aside all material considerations, should be understood to be bona fide spiritual masters.

title: a-brahma-bhuvanal lokah
translation: All planets subject to rebirth

word_by_word.meaning: up to the abode of Brahma

title: yasya prabha prabhavato jagad-anda-koti
translation: Brahmajyoti as Krishna's effulgence

title: tasyaiva hetoh prayateta kovido
translation: Persons who are actually intelligent and philosophically inclined should endeavor only for that purposeful end which is not obtainable even by wandering from the topmost plane (Brahmaloka) down to the lowest planet (Patala). As far as happiness derived from sense enjoyment is concerned, it can be obtained automatically in course of time, just as in course of time we obtain miseries even though we do not desire them.

word_by_word.meaning: as a result of previous work

title: cintamani-prakara-sadmasu kalpa-vrksa
translation: Description of Goloka Vrndavana

title: jnane prayasam udapasya namanta eva
translation: Those who, even while remaining situated in their established social positions, throw away the process of speculative knowledge and with their body, words and mind offer all respects to descriptions of Your personality and activities, dedicating their lives to these narrations, which are vibrated by You personally and by Your pure devotees, certainly conquer Your Lordship, although You are otherwise unconquerable by anyone within the three worlds.

title: urdhvam gacchanti sattva-stha
translation: Mode of goodness elevates

word_by_word.meaning: those situated in the mode of goodness
those in the mode of passion
those in the mode of ignorance

title: apareyam itas tv anyam
translation: Superior energy — living entities

title: yasya prasadad bhagavat-prasado
translation: Guru's mercy is Krishna's mercy

title: raso 'ham apsu kaunteya
translation: Krishna as taste of water

word_by_word.meaning: of the moon and the sun

title: satatam kirtayanto mam
translation: Great souls always chant

title: ksipram bhavati dharmatma
translation: Quickly becomes righteous

title: brahma satyam jagan mithya
translation: World is illusion — Shankara

title: yam yam vapi smaran bhavam
translation: State of mind at death

title: mahatmanas tu mam partha
translation: Great souls always worship Krishna

title: api cet su-duracaro
translation: Even a miscreant is saintly if devoted

title: annad bhavanti bhutani
translation: Food from rain from sacrifice

title: jalaja nava-laksani
translation: 8.4 million species of life

title: prayen alpayusah sabhya
translation: O learned one, in this iron age of Kali men have but short lives. They are quarrelsome, lazy, misguided, unlucky and, above all, always disturbed.

title: yan maithunadi-grhamedhi-sukham
translation: Sex life is compared to the rubbing of two hands to relieve an itch. Grhamedhis, so-called grhasthas who have no spiritual knowledge, think that this itching is the greatest platform of happiness, although actually it is a source of distress. The krpanas, the fools who are just the opposite of brahmanas, are not satisfied by repeated sensuous enjoyment. Those who are dhira, however, who are sober and who tolerate this itching, are not subjected to the sufferings of fools and rascals.

word_by_word.meaning: that which (is meant for material sense gratification)
represented by talking of sex, reading sexual literature or enjoying sex life (at home or outside, as in a club)
all types of material happiness based on attachment to family, society, friendship, etc.
of the two hands (to relieve the itching)
different types of unhappiness (into which one is put after such itching sense gratification)
subjected to various types of material unhappiness
if one can learn from such itching
which is simply a mental concoction (actually there is no happiness)
(he can become) a most perfect, sober person

title: paras tasmat tu bhavo 'nyo
translation: Unmanifest eternal beyond the unmanifest

title: yam brahma-varunendra-rudra-marutah
transliteration: sūta uvāca yaṁ brahmā varuṇendra-rudra-marutaḥ stunvanti divyaiḥ stavair vedaiḥ sāṅga-pada-kramopaniṣadair gāyanti yaṁ
sāma-gāḥ dhyānāvasthita-tad-gatena manasā paśyanti yaṁ yogino yasyāntaṁ na viduḥ surāsura-gaṇā devāya tasmai namaḥ
translation: Sūta Gosvāmī said: Unto that personality whom Brahmā, Varuṇa, Indra, Rudra and the Maruts praise by chanting transcendental hymns and reciting the Vedas with all their corollaries, pada-kramas and Upaniṣads, to whom the chanters of the Sāma Veda always sing, whom the perfected yogīs see within their minds after fixing themselves in trance and absorbing themselves within Him, and whose limit can never be found by any demigod or demon — unto that Supreme Personality of Godhead I offer my humble obeisances.

title: sa vai manah krsna-padaravindayor
translation: Maharaja Ambarisa always engaged his mind in meditating upon the lotus feet of Krsna, his words in describing the glories of the Lord, his hands in cleansing the Lord’s temple, and his ears in hearing the words spoken by Krsna or about Krsna. He engaged his eyes in seeing the Deity of Krsna, Krsna’s temples and Krsna’s places like Mathura and Vrndavana, he engaged his sense of touch in touching the bodies of the Lord’s devotees, he engaged his sense of smell in smelling the fragrance of tulasi offered to the Lord, and he engaged his tongue in tasting the Lord’s prasada. He engaged his legs in walking to the holy places and temples of the Lord, his head in bowing down before the Lord, and all his desires in serving the Lord, twenty-four hours a day. Indeed, Maharaja Ambarisa never desired anything for his own sense gratification. He engaged all his senses in devotional service, in various engagements related to the Lord. This is the way to increase attachment for the Lord and be completely free from all material desires.

word_by_word.meaning: (fixed) upon the two lotus feet of Lord Krsna
in activities like cleansing the temple of Hari, the Supreme Personality of Godhead
of or about Krsna, who never falls down
in seeing the Deity and temples and holy dhamas of Mukunda
in the prasada offered to the Lord
holy places like the temple or Vrndavana and Dvaraka
of Krsna, the master of the senses
in offering obeisances to the lotus feet
in being engaged as a servant
with a desire for sense gratification
if one takes shelter of a devotee such as Prahlada

title: matra-sparsas tu kaunteya
translation: Tolerate happiness and distress

word_by_word.meaning: contact of the senses with the sense objects

title: acintya-bhedabheda-tattva
translation: Inconceivable simultaneous difference and oneness

title: krsi-go-raksya-vanijyam
translation: Vaisya duties — farming cow protection trade

word_by_word.meaning: of the mercantile and farming class

title: sa evayam maya te 'dya
translation: That same ancient yoga revealed to you

title: na dhanam na janam na sundarim
transliteration: na dhanaṁ na janaṁ na sundarīṁ
translation: “ ‘O Lord of the universe, I do not desire material wealth, materialistic followers, a beautiful wife or fruitive activities described in flowery language. All I want, life after life, is unmotivated devotional service to You.’

word_by_word.meaning: fruitive activities described in flowery language
unto the Supreme Personality of Godhead

title: tat sadhu manye 'sura-varya dehinam
translation: Prahlada Maharaja replied: O best of the asuras, King of the demons, as far as I have learned from my spiritual master, any person who has accepted a temporary body and temporary household life is certainly embarrassed by anxiety because of having fallen in a dark well where there is no water but only suffering. One should give up this position and go to the forest (vana). More clearly, one should go to Vrndavana, where only Krsna consciousness is prevalent, and should thus take shelter of the Supreme Personality of Godhead.

word_by_word.meaning: very good, or the best part of life
of persons who have accepted the material body
because of accepting the temporary body or bodily relations as real (thinking I am this body, and everything belonging to this body is min)
the place where spiritual culture or self-realization is stopped
the bodily concept of life, or household life
which is nothing but a blind well (where there is no water but one nonetheless searches for water)

title: yasyaika-nisvasita-kalam athavalambhya
translation: Maha-Vishnu breathes out universes

title: srotavyadini rajendra
translation: Those persons who are materially engrossed, being blind to the knowledge of ultimate truth, leave many subject matters for hearing in human society, O Emperor.

word_by_word.meaning: knowledge of self, the ultimate truth

title: atha va bahunaitena
translation: Krishna pervades entire universe with fragment

title: kaler dosa-nidhe rajan
translation: My dear King, although Kali-yuga is an ocean of faults, there is still one good quality about this age: Simply by chanting the Hare Kṛṣṇa mahā-mantra, one can become free from material bondage and be promoted to the transcendental kingdom.

title: namo maha-vadanyaya
translation: “O most munificent incarnation! You are Kṛṣṇa Himself appearing as Śrī Kṛṣṇa Caitanya Mahāprabhu. You have assumed the golden color of Śrīmatī Rādhārāṇī, and You are widely distributing pure love of Kṛṣṇa. We offer our respectful obeisances unto You.

word_by_word.meaning: who is most munificent and charitably disposed
whose complexion is the golden complexion of Srimati Radharani

title: visaya vinivartante
translation: Sense objects withdrawn by higher taste

title: nehabhikrama-naso 'sti
translation: No loss in devotional service

title: nasta-prayesu abhadresu
translation: By regular attendance in classes on the Bhagavatam and by rendering of service to the pure devotee, all that is troublesome to the heart is almost completely destroyed, and loving service unto the Personality of Godhead, who is praised with transcendental songs, is established as an irrevocable fact.

title: samasrita ye pada-pallava-plavam
transliteration: padaṁpadaṁ padaṁ yad vipadāṁ na teṣām
translation: For those who have accepted the boat of the lotus feet of the Lord, who is the shelter of the cosmic manifestation and is famous as Murāri, the enemy of the Mura demon, the ocean of the material world is like the water contained in a calf’s hoof-print. Their goal is paraṁ padam, Vaikuṇṭha, the place where there are no material miseries, not the place where there is danger at every step.

title: ksetra-jnam capi mam vidhi
translation: Krishna as knower in all bodies

word_by_word.meaning: of the knower of the field

title: na me viduh sura-ganah
translation: Neither gods nor sages know Krishna's origin

title: asocyan anvasocas tvam
translation: Wise grieve for neither living nor dead

title: janmana jayate sudrah
translation: Born a sudra — brahmin by quality

title: tapasa brahmacaryena
translation: To concentrate the mind, one must observe a life of celibacy and not fall down. One must undergo the austerity of voluntarily giving up sense enjoyment. One must then control the mind and senses, give charity, be truthful, clean and nonviolent, follow the regulative principles and regularly chant the holy name of the Lord. Thus a sober and faithful person who knows the religious principles is temporarily purified of all sins performed with his body, words and mind. These sins are like the dried leaves of creepers beneath a bamboo tree, which may be burned by fire although their roots remain to grow again at the first opportunity.

word_by_word.meaning: by austerity or voluntary rejection of material enjoyment
by voluntarily giving charity to good causes
and by following regulative principles to keep oneself internally and externally clean
by regularly chanting the holy name of the Lord
performed by the body, words and intelligence
fully imbued with knowledge of religious principles
the dried creepers beneath a bamboo tree

title: kamasya nendriya-pritir
translation: Life’s desires should never be directed toward sense gratification. One should desire only a healthy life, or self-preservation, since a human being is meant for inquiry about the Absolute Truth. Nothing else should be the goal of one’s works.

title: kamais tais tair hrta-jnanah
translation: Those whose judgment is stolen worship demigods

word_by_word.meaning: whose knowledge has been carried away

title: naivodvije para duratyaya-vaitaranyas
translation: O best of the great personalities, I am not at all afraid of material existence, for wherever I stay I am fully absorbed in thoughts of Your glories and activities. My concern is only for the fools and rascals who are making elaborate plans for material happiness and maintaining their families, societies and countries. I am simply concerned with love for them.

word_by_word.meaning: insurmountable or very difficult to cross
of the Vaitarani, the river of the material world
of Your Lordship’s glories and activities
in the great ocean of nectarean spiritual bliss
the fools and rascals who are bereft of Krsna consciousness
the false burden or responsibility (of maintaining one’s family, society and nation and elaborate arrangements for that purpose)
who are lifting (by making grand plans for this arrangement)
although all of them are nothing but fools and rascals (I am thinking of them also)

title: naham prakasah sarvasya
translation: Krishna not manifest to all

title: prapya punya-krtam lokan
translation: Unsuccessful yogi returns to pious family

title: prthivite ache yata nagaradi-grama
translation: Chanting in every town and village

title: anasaktasya visayan
translation: Yukta-vairagya — use everything for Krishna

title: aisvarasya samagrasya
translation: Six opulences of the Lord

title: varnasramacaravata
translation: Varnasrama meant to satisfy Vishnu

title: yasya deve para bhaktir
translation: Knowledge manifests for one devoted to God

title: catur-vidha bhajante mam
translation: Four kinds of pious who approach Krishna

word_by_word.meaning: those who are situated in knowledge
The best amongst the Bharatas, Arjun

title: sarvam khalv idam brahma
translation: Everything is Brahman

title: kiba vipra kiba nyasi sudra kene naya
translation: “Whether one is a brāhmaṇa, a sannyāsī or a śūdra — regardless of what he is — he can become a spiritual master if he knows the science of Kṛṣṇa.”

word_by_word.meaning: a knower of the science of Krsna

title: yad yad acarati sresthah
translation: Leaders set the standard for others

title: anarthopasamam saksat
translation: The material miseries of the living entity, which are superfluous to him, can be directly mitigated by the linking process of devotional service. But the mass of people do not know this, and therefore the learned Vyasadeva compiled this Vedic literature, which is in relation to the Supreme Truth.

word_by_word.meaning: the linking process of devotional service
of the general mass of men
in relation with the Supreme Truth

title: nitya-siddha krsna-prema
translation: “Pure love for Kṛṣṇa is eternally established in the hearts of the living entities. It is not something to be gained from another source. When the heart is purified by hearing and chanting, this love naturally awakens.

title: amanitvam adambhitvam
translation: Humility as knowledge

title: nasto mohah smrtir labdha
translation: Arjuna's illusion dispelled

title: kirata-hunandhra-pulinda-pulkasa
translation: Kirata, Huna, Andhra, Pulinda, Pulkasa, Abhira, Sumbha, Yavana, members of the Khasa races and even others addicted to sinful acts can be purified by taking shelter of the devotees of the Lord, due to His being the supreme power. I beg to offer my respectful obeisances unto Him.

word_by_word.meaning: having taken shelter of the devotees of the Lord

title: mahat-sevam dvaram ahur vimuktes
translation: One can attain the path of liberation from material bondage only by rendering service to highly advanced spiritual personalities. These personalities are impersonalists and devotees. Whether one wants to merge into the Lord’s existence or wants to associate with the Personality of Godhead, one should render service to the mahatmas. For those who are not interested in such activities, who associate with people fond of women and sex, the path to hell is wide open. The mahatmas are equipoised. They do not see any difference between one living entity and another. They are very peaceful and are fully engaged in devotional service. They are devoid of anger, and they work for the benefit of everyone. They do not behave in any abominable way. Such people are known as mahatmas.

word_by_word.meaning: service to the spiritually advanced persons called mahatmas
the way to the dungeon of a dark, hellish condition of life
persons who see everyone in a spiritual identity
very peaceful, situated in Brahman or Bhagavan
without anger (one must distribute Krsna consciousness to persons who are hostile without becoming angry at them)

title: adau sraddha tatah sadhu-sanga
translation: Stages of devotional development

title: tada rajas-tamo-bhavah
translation: As soon as irrevocable loving service is established in the heart, the effects of nature’s modes of passion and ignorance, such as lust, desire and hankering, disappear from the heart. Then the devotee is established in goodness, and he becomes completely happy.

title: ajo 'pi sann avyayatma
translation: Krishna appears though unborn

title: asraddadhanah purusa
translation: Without faith no devotional service

title: yac-caksur esa savita sakala-grahanam
translation: Sun as Krishna's eye

title: sva-vid-varahostra-kharaih
translation: Men who are like dogs, hogs, camels and asses praise those men who never listen to the transcendental pastimes of Lord Sri Krsna, the deliverer from evils.

word_by_word.meaning: the village hog who eats stool
Lord Krsna, the deliver from all evils

title: ananyas cintayanto mam
translation: Krishna provides for those always devoted

title: akamah sarva-kamo va
translation: A person who has broader intelligence, whether he be full of all material desire, without any material desire, or desiring liberation, must by all means worship the supreme whole, the Personality of Godhead.

word_by_word.meaning: one who has transcended all material desires
one who has the sum total of material desires
by devotional service to the Lord

title: satam prasangam mama virya-samvido
translation: In the association of pure devotees, discussion of the pastimes and activities of the Supreme Personality of Godhead is very pleasing and satisfying to the ear and the heart. By cultivating such knowledge one gradually becomes advanced on the path of liberation, and thereafter he is freed, and his attraction becomes fixed. Then real devotion and devotional service begin.

title: krte yad dhyayato visnum
translation: Whatever result was obtained in Satya-yuga by meditating on Viṣṇu, in Tretā-yuga by performing sacrifices, and in Dvāpara-yuga by serving the Lord’s lotus feet can be obtained in Kali-yuga simply by chanting the Hare Kṛṣṇa mahā-mantra.

title: srsti-sthiti-pralaya-sadhana-saktir eka
translation: Durga as shadow energy

title: ittham satam brahma-sukhanubhutya
translation: All the boys would be differently engaged. Some boys blew their flutes, and others blew bugles made of horn. Some imitated the buzzing of the bumblebees, and others imitated the voice of the cuckoo. Some boys imitated flying birds by running after the birds’ shadows on the ground, some imitated the beautiful movements and attractive postures of the swans, some sat down with the ducks, sitting silently, and others imitated the dancing of the peacocks. Some boys attracted young monkeys in the trees, some jumped into the trees, imitating the monkeys, some made faces as the monkeys were accustomed to do, and others jumped from one branch to another. Some boys went to the waterfalls and crossed over the river, jumping with the frogs, and when they saw their own reflections on the water they would laugh. They would also condemn the sounds of their own echoes. In this way, all the cowherd boys used to play with Kṛṣṇa, who is the source of the Brahman effulgence for jñānīs desiring to merge into that effulgence, who is the Supreme Personality of Godhead for devotees who have accepted eternal servitorship, and who for ordinary persons is but another ordinary child. The cowherd boys, having accumulated the results of pious activities for many lives, were able to associate in this way with the Supreme Personality of Godhead. How can one explain their great fortune?

title: svayambhur narada sambhuh
translation: Lord Brahma, Bhagavan Narada, Lord Siva, the four Kumaras, Lord Kapila (the son of Devahuti), Svayambhuva Manu, Prahlada Maharaja, Janaka Maharaja, Grandfather Bhisma, Bali Maharaja, Sukadeva Gosvami and I myself know the real religious principle. My dear servants, this transcendental religious principle, which is known as bhagavata-dharma, or surrender unto the Supreme Lord and love for Him, is uncontaminated by the material modes of nature. It is very confidential and difficult for ordinary human beings to understand, but if by chance one fortunately understands it, he is immediately liberated, and thus he returns home, back to Godhead.

word_by_word.meaning: which teach a person how to love the Supreme Personality of Godhead
transcendental, not contaminated by the material modes of nature

title: raja-vidya raja-guhyam
translation: King of knowledge king of secrets

title: prapancikataya buddhya
translation: Not to be used for material purposes

title: gurur na sa syat sva-jano na sa syat
transliteration: syātpitā na sa syājjananī na sā syāt
translation: One who cannot deliver his dependents from the path of repeated birth and death should never become a spiritual master, a father, a husband, a mother or a worshipable demigod.

word_by_word.meaning: one who is on the path of repeated birth and death

title: yah sastra-vidhim utsrjya
translation: Acting against scripture attains no perfection

title: ramadi-murtisu kala-niyamena tisthan
translation: Krishna expands as Rama etc. by His will

title: saksad-dharitvena samasta-sastrair
translation: Guru is honored as Krishna by scriptures

title: bhaktih paresanubhavo viraktir
translation: Devotion, direct experience of the Supreme Lord, and detachment from other things — these three occur simultaneously for one who has taken shelter of the Supreme Personality of Godhead, in the same way that pleasure, nourishment and relief from hunger come simultaneously and increasingly, with each bite, for a person engaged in eating.

title: parabhavas tavad abodha-jato
translation: As long as one does not inquire about the spiritual values of life, one is defeated and subjected to miseries arising from ignorance. Be it sinful or pious, karma has its resultant actions. If a person is engaged in any kind of karma, his mind is called karmatmaka, colored with fruitive activity. As long as the mind is impure, consciousness is unclear, and as long as one is absorbed in fruitive activity, he has to accept a material body.

title: namo brahmanya-devaya
translation: Obeisances to Vishnu protector of brahmins

title: titiksavah karunikah
translation: The symptoms of a sadhu are that he is tolerant, merciful and friendly to all living entities. He has no enemies, he is peaceful, he abides by the scriptures, and all his characteristics are sublime.
